Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"Managing and addressing the proliferation of fake content on social media is not just a technical challenge; it’s also a moral imperative that requires vigilance and a proactive approach. As someone who spends several hours daily on these platforms, I’ve seen firsthand how misleading information can spread rapidly, influencing public opinion and even sparking real-world conflicts. To combat this, I believe the most effective strategy involves a combination of personal awareness, community engagement, and leveraging technological tools.", "reasoning_summary":"My approach starts with critical thinking. Before sharing or reacting to any content on social media, I ask myself three key questions: Who created this? What evidence supports it? Could there be another perspective? This reflects my curious nature and the conscientiousness trait of wanting to understand the world better. For example, if a friend shares an alarming post about health claims, I research the source and check for credible references before passing it along. Additionally, I engage with peers who share similar concerns about misinformation, fostering discussions that help us all become more discerning consumers.", "tone":"inquisitive yet practical", "citations":["Pew Research Center"],"safety_notes":"","used_persona_fields":["age","gender","occupation","time_onsocialmedia"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Managing and addressing the proliferation of fake content on social media is not just a technical challenge; it’s also a moral imperative that requires vigilance and a proactive approach. As someone who spends several hours daily on these platforms, I’ve seen firsthand how misleading information can spread rapidly, influencing public opinion and even sparking real-world conflicts. To combat this, I believe the most effective strategy involves a combination of personal awareness, community engagement, and leveraging technological tools.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: My approach starts with critical thinking. Before sharing or reacting to any content on social media, I ask myself three key questions: Who created this? What evidence supports it? Could there be another perspective? This reflects my curious nature and the conscientiousness trait of wanting to understand the world better. For example, if a friend shares an alarming post about health claims, I research the source and check for credible references before passing it along. Additionally, I engage with peers who share similar concerns about misinformation, fostering discussions that help us all become more discerning consumers.
